CREATE TABLE test_timezones (id INT, datetime_value DATETIME);
INSERT INTO test_timezones (id, datetime_value) VALUES (1, CAST('2021-01-01 12:00:00' AS DATETIME));
INSERT INTO test_timezones (id, datetime_value) VALUES (2, CAST('2021-06-15 20:30:00' AS DATETIME));
SELECT adjust_timezone(datetime_value, -5) FROM test_timezones WHERE id = 1;
SELECT adjust_timezone(datetime_value, 3) FROM test_timezones WHERE id = 2;
SELECT adjust_timezone(CAST('2023-03-15 12:00:00' AS DATETIME), 0, 2) AS new_time WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_timezones WHERE id = 1);
SELECT adjust_timezone(CAST('2023-03-15 12:00:00' AS DATETIME), 7) FROM test_timezones WHERE datetime_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT adjust_timezone(datetime_value, 1) FROM test_timezones WHERE datetime_value IS NOT NULL AND id IN (SELECT id FROM test_timezones WHERE id < 3);
SELECT adjust_timezone(CAST('2015-12-31 23:59:59' AS DATETIME), -1) AS adjusted_time FROM test_timezones;
SELECT adjust_timezone(CAST('2010-01-01 00:00:00' AS DATETIME), -12, 0) FROM test_timezones WHERE id = 1;
SELECT adjust_timezone(CAST('2024-02-29 00:00:00' AS DATETIME), 2) FROM test_timezones WHERE id = 1;
SELECT adjust_timezone(CAST('2023-07-04 14:00:00' AS DATETIME), 6) FROM (SELECT datetime_value FROM test_timezones) AS subquery;
SELECT adjust_timezone(CAST('2023-12-31 23:59:59' AS DATETIME), 99) AS extreme_timezone_adjustment;
SELECT adjust_timezone(CAST('2023-01-01 12:00:00' AS DATETIME), 0, 0) FROM test_timezones;
